Carecas do ABC.description: Brazilian skinhead group based in the ABC region of São Paulo, founded in 1987 as a dissident from Carecas do Subúrbio. The group accepts Afro-Brazilian members and declares itself nationalist and anti-communist, but uses swastikas and Nazi greetings. Members have been accused of several murders, including the beating death of Edson Néris da Silva. The group currently has 250 members and is less active than in the 1980s and 1990s.

descriptions: Carecas do ABC split from Carecas do Subúrbio as a dissident faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Carecas do ABC emerged in 1987 as a dissident from the group Carecas do Subúrbio.

descriptions: Carecas do ABC is a dissident faction that split from Carecas do Subúrbio.
edges.evidence_quote: Carecas do ABC são uma gangue skinhead dissidente do grupo Carecas do Subúrbio

Dödspatrullen.description: Swedish Somali organized crime syndicate originating from Rinkeby, Stockholm, founded in 2015. Engaged in illegal drug trade, murders, and other criminal activities. Operates in Sweden, Finland, Denmark, Morocco, and Spain. Known for a long-running gang war with rival group Shottaz since 2015.

descriptions: Dödspatrullen and Shottaz have been in a gang war since 2015, resulting in multiple deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: Dödspatrullen has been in a gang war with Shottaz, another Swedish Somali criminal organization originating from Rinkeby, since 2015.

67.description: 67 is a British music group and street gang in the UK drill scene, originating from Brixton Hill, London. Formed in 2012, they are early adopters of the UK drill sound and gained mainstream popularity with tracks like 'Let's Lurk'. The group has been labelled a criminal gang by the Metropolitan Police and faced bans on performances. They are involved in drug trafficking and have several members with criminal records.

descriptions: 150 had a rivalry with 67.
edges.evidence_quote: 150 would develop a rivalry with Brixton Hill-based group 67.

descriptions: A member of 67 killed a member of 150 in a gang dispute.
edges.evidence_quote: On 25 February 2015, a member of the drill group and street gang, 150, Dwayne Simpson aka SQ, was stabbed to death by a member of 67 over a gang dispute.

descriptions: Cheyon Evans, associated with 37/OJB, was killed by a 67 member, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Cheyon Evans was known as 'Stompz' and allegedly was a part of the 37/OJB gang, with him also having ties to the CT gang based in Clapham, one of 67's main rivals.

descriptions: CT is described as one of 67's main rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Cheyon Evans was known as 'Stompz' and allegedly was a part of the 37/OJB gang, with him also having ties to the CT gang based in Clapham, one of 67's main rivals.

Charlestown Gang.description: Irish American organized crime gang based in Charlestown, Boston, headed by the McLaughlin brothers. Active from the 1950s until 1966, the gang controlled gambling and loan sharking on the docks and carried out contract killings for the Patriarca and Genovese crime families. They were destroyed in a gang war with the Winter Hill Gang, resulting in the deaths or imprisonment of its leaders.

descriptions: The Charlestown Gang performed contract killings for the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The McLaughlin brothers forged ties with the Mafia, carrying out contract killings for the Patriarca crime family in Boston and the Genovese crime family in New York City.

descriptions: The Charlestown Gang performed contract killings for the Patriarca crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The McLaughlin brothers forged ties with the Mafia, carrying out contract killings for the Patriarca crime family in Boston and the Genovese crime family in New York City.

descriptions: The two gangs were engaged in a violent gang war from 1961 onward.
edges.evidence_quote: The Charlestown Gang became involved in a gang war with a rival Irish mob group, the Winter Hill Gang of Somerville led by James 'Buddy' McLean, beginning in 1961.

descriptions: Charlestown Gang listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang, Gustin Gang, Winter Hill Gang, and various other gangs in New England

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang defeated the McLaughlin Gang in a gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: After vanquishing the McLaughlin Gang of Charlestown

descriptions: Infobx lists Charlestown Gang as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang defeated the McLaughlin Gang in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: After vanquishing the McLaughlin Gang of Charlestown

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang defeated the McLaughlin Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: After vanquishing the McLaughlin Gang of Charlestown

descriptions: Duroc is listed as a rival of Azusa 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Duroc

descriptions: Finnish Resistance Movement cooperated with Feuerkrieg Division.
edges.evidence_quote: The Finnish Resistance Movement and its successor Atomwaffen Finland cooperated with the group as well.

descriptions: Abete clan was an ally of Scissionisti.
edges.evidence_quote: Abete clan (Scampia) - Boss: Arcangelo Abete

Harlem Spartans.description: British hip-hop collective based in Kennington, London, formed in 2014. Known for pioneering UK drill music, with members accumulating over 100 million streams. Police allege it is a gang, but the group denies this. Has a notable rivalry with Brixton group 410.

descriptions: Harlem Spartans has a rivalry with 410, expressed through diss tracks and physical violence.
edges.evidence_quote: The group is known for its rivalry with Brixton -based group 410.

descriptions: Harlem Spartans is affiliated with Moscow17.
edges.evidence_quote: Harlem Spartans is affiliated with Walworth -based group Moscow17.

descriptions: Onefour cites Harlem Spartans as a major influence.
edges.evidence_quote: Australian drill group Onefour cites Harlem Spartans as a major influence in their music.

Four Seas Gang.description: Taiwanese triad society founded in May 1954 by mainland Chinese refugees who fled with the KMT. Active in alien smuggling in Southern California, with estimated membership of 10,000 in Taiwan and branches in Los Angeles and Santa Ana. Uses grey as its color.

descriptions: After the Golden Dragon massacre, Wah Ching drove the Hop Sing out of San Francisco.
edges.evidence_quote: In the wake of the 1977 shooting, the Wah Ching were ascendant and the Hop Sing were chased out of San Francisco.

descriptions: Hop Sing Boys is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wah Ching , Hop Sing Boys

descriptions: The Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged under an alliance led by Peter Chong and Raymond Chow.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the alliance, the Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged, and an umbrella organization, Tien Hu Wui, was formed to oversee their combined business.

Abu Sayyaf.description: Jihadist militant and pirate group based in the Philippines, founded in 1991 by Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani. It follows Wahhabi Sunni Islam and has been responsible for bombings, kidnappings, and assassinations, including the 2004 MV Superferry 14 bombing. The group aligned with the Islamic State in 2014. As of 2023, its membership had dwindled to about 20, and it was declared dismantled in 2024 but resurfaced in 2026.

descriptions: 14K cooperates with Abu Sayyaf in arms smuggling and money laundering.
edges.evidence_quote: The 14K triad has been involved in smuggling arms to Abu Sayyaf and has reportedly cooperated with the Islamic group in laundering and transmitting ransom money, taking a percentage of the ransoms in exchange for their assistance.

descriptions: 14K is listed as an ally of Abu Sayyaf in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Maute Group, Al-Qaeda (formerly)

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf coordinated with 14K Triad for kidnappings and received military support from them.
edges.evidence_quote: Abu Sayyaf coordinated with the Chinese 14K Triad gang in carrying out the kidnappings. The 14K Triad has militarily supported Abu Sayyaf.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda provided initial funding to Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: The government noted that initial funding for ASG came from al-Qaeda through the brother-in-law of Osama bin Laden, Mohammed Jamal Khalifa.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf claimed that Daulat Ul-Islamiya, an allied group, was responsible for the Davao City bombing.
edges.evidence_quote: Abu Sayyaf spokesperson Abu Rami was reported to claim responsibility. He later denied the report and any involvement, saying a group allied to them; the Daulat Ul-Islamiya were responsible.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf pledged allegiance to Islamic State, becoming its East Asia Province.
edges.evidence_quote: On July 23, 2014, Isnilon Hapilon, one of the group's leaders, swore an oath of loyalty to Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi, the leader of the Islamic State (IS) officially aligning the group with the global jihadist movement.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf trained with Jemaah Islamiyah.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was reported to have trained alongside Jemaah Islamiyah militants.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf fought alongside the Maute Group in the Battle of Marawi.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2017, Hapilon and other members of Abu Sayyaf joined the Islamic extremist Maute Group based in Lanao del Sur during their attempt to seize control of Marawi City.

descriptions: MILF condemned Abu Sayyaf and labeled them 'anti-Islam'.
edges.evidence_quote: The terrorism against civilians committed were condemned by MNLF and MILF, who said that Abu Sayyaf strayed from their real paths of struggle, with MILF labeling Abu Sayyaf as 'anti-Islam'

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf recruited members from MILF.
edges.evidence_quote: Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani's first recruits were soldiers of the MNLF and the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF).

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf and renegade MILF members were suspected of jointly kidnapping a priest.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 11, 2009, Irish Catholic missionary Michael Sinnott, aged 79, from Barntown County Wexford was kidnapped from a gated compound in Pagadian, the capital of Zamboanga del Sur province, suspected to be part of ASG and some renegade members of MILF.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf recruited members from MNLF.
edges.evidence_quote: Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani's first recruits were soldiers of the MNLF and the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF).

descriptions: Ajang Ajang group is a cell within Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: The Ajang Ajang group (...) was a cell within the terrorist organization Abu Sayyaf.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Abu Sayyaf (pledged allegiance to ISIL in 2014)

descriptions: JI considered Abu Sayyaf an opponent until 2024 according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: State opponents United Nations, Malaysia, Indonesia, Non-state opponents Moro National Liberation Front, Moro Islamic Liberation Front, Free Papua Movement, Free Aceh Movement (until 2005), Abu Sayyaf (until 2024)

descriptions: JI has links with ASG.
edges.evidence_quote: Recruiting, training, indoctrination, financial, and operational links between the JI and other militant groups, such as al-Qaeda, the Abu Sayyaf Group (ASG), the Misuari Renegade/Breakaway Group (MRG/MBG) and the Philippine Rajah Sulaiman movement (RSM) have existed for many years.

descriptions: Sigue Sigue Commando and Abu Sayyaf formed an alliance during the prison riots.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang retaliated against an alliance of Sigue Sigue Commando and Abu Sayyaf convicts

Primeiro Grupo Catarinense.description: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense (PGC) is the largest criminal organization in the state of Santa Catarina, Brazil, founded on March 3, 2003, in the Florianópolis prison. It commands rebellions, robberies, kidnappings, murders, and drug trafficking, operating primarily in Santa Catarina with extensions into at least three other Brazilian states and borders with Paraguay, Bolivia, and Peru. The group has 14 leaders divided into two ministries and is modeled after the PCC and Comando Vermelho.

descriptions: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense is listed as an ally of Bala na Cara in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Comando Vermelho , Primeiro Grupo Catarinense , Os Aberto

descriptions: Infobox lists Primeiro Grupo Catarinense as an ally of CV.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense [pt] , Bala na Cara [pt]

descriptions: Infobox lists Primeiro Grupo Catarinense as an ally of Comando Vermelho.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense

descriptions: PGC is listed as an ally of Os Manos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: PCC , PGC

descriptions: PCC lists Primeiro Grupo Catarinense as a rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Comando Vermelho , Família do Norte , Militias , Okaida [ pt ] , Bala na Cara [ pt ] , Primeiro Grupo Catarinense [ pt ] , Sindicato do Crime [ pt ] , Clan Rotela

descriptions: ADA is listed as a rival of PGC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivais: PCC , ADA , OSM

descriptions: BNC is listed as an ally of PGC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: CV , BNC

descriptions: PGC is allied with Comando Vermelho.
edges.evidence_quote: It is known for their alliance with § Comando Vermelho

descriptions: PGC allied with Comando Vermelho for information exchange and training in weapons handling and crime sophistication.
edges.evidence_quote: A principal delas foi com o Comando Vermelho, liderado pelo narcotraficante Fernandinho Beira-Mar, a quem coube, além de trocas de informações, o aperfeiçoamento dos recrutados pelo PGC no manuseio de armas e de fuzis e a sofisticação no modus operandi dos crimes.

descriptions: OSM is listed as a rival of PGC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivais: PCC , ADA , OSM

descriptions: PGC has an ongoing rivalry with PCC.
edges.evidence_quote: its ongoing rivalry with § Primeiro Comando da Capital

descriptions: PGC entered a war against PCC due to its alliance with Comando Vermelho, which is a rival of PCC.
edges.evidence_quote: A aliança entre as duas facções causou a entrada do PGC na Guerra contra o PCC, rival da facção fluminense.

descriptions: The Order II grew out of the original Order, attempting to perpetuate its activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Order II (Bruder Schweigen Strike Force II) was also anti-government and antisemitic.

descriptions: Sombra Negra considered FMLN an enemy during the civil war.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: FMLN ( Durante la Guerra Civil )

descriptions: Los Páez are a cell allied with Cártel de Caborca, as they are under the command of Caro Quintero's nephew and are in conflict with Sinaloa-affiliated cells.
edges.evidence_quote: creciente conflicto entre células delincuenciales Los Páez y/o Los Paleteros (al mando Rodrigo Páez Quintero sobrino de Rafael Caro Quintero) y Los Cazadores junto a Los Salazar

descriptions: Los Páez is a cell led by a nephew of the Caborca Cartel leader, likely allied.
edges.evidence_quote: criminal cells Los Páez and/or Los Paleteros (led by Rodrigo Páez Quintero, nephew of Rafael Caro Quintero)

descriptions: Ferone clan switched sides to join the Scissionisti.
edges.evidence_quote: dopo tale episodio il clan Ferone passerà nelle file degli Scissionisti

descriptions: Ferone clan was initially allied with Di Lauro.
edges.evidence_quote: affiliati al clan Ferone (clan vicino ai Di Lauro)

'Ndrina Lo Presti.description: The Lo Presti 'ndrina is a Mafia clan of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Marina di Gioiosa Jonica in Calabria and established in Bardonecchia, Piedmont, in the late 1950s. Initially part of the Mazzaferro family, it became an independent 'ndrina. It is known for infiltration of public administration, leading to the dissolution of the Bardonecchia city council in 1995. The clan has been involved in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, and usury.

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Alvaro.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Barbaro.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Belfiore and many other Calabrian and Sicilian mafia families.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Members of Clan Cataldo were arrested alongside Lo Presti members in 1993.
edges.evidence_quote: Vengono coinvolti e arrestati anche esponenti del clan Cataldo di Locri .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Cataldo.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti's successor Luciano Ursino entered business relations with the Crea brothers, bosses of the Turin 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Luciano Ursino, entra in rapporti di affari con i fratelli Adolfo e Aldo Cosimo Crea, originari di Stilo , in Calabria , boss emergenti della 'Ndrangheta torinese.

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Facchineri.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Ierinò.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti and Mazzaferro are jointly hegemonic in Val di Susa.
edges.evidence_quote: È la famiglia della 'Ndrangheta egemone per eccellenza sul territorio della Val di Susa insieme a quella dei Mazzaferro .

descriptions: Lo Presti was originally part of Mazzaferro, then became a faction and later an independent 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: Nata inizialmente come intranea alla famiglia Mazzaferro , diventa una fazione e successivamente 'ndrina a sé.

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Mesiani Mazzacuva.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Morabito.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Palamara.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti formed alliances with the Racco 'ndrina in Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto rapporti di affari con la Famiglia Inzerillo -Spatola-Gambino di Palermo , attraverso la quale strinse alleanze anche oltreoceano con la Famiglia Gambino di New York e con le famiglie della 'Ndrangheta in Canada degli Zito , dei Commisso , dei Racco, degli Stalteri a Toronto e dei Cotroni a Montréal .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Raso-Albanese.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti formed alliances with the Stalteri 'ndrina in Toronto.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto rapporti di affari con la Famiglia Inzerillo -Spatola-Gambino di Palermo , attraverso la quale strinse alleanze anche oltreoceano con la Famiglia Gambino di New York e con le famiglie della 'Ndrangheta in Canada degli Zito , dei Commisso , dei Racco, degli Stalteri a Toronto e dei Cotroni a Montréal .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Strangio.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Tripodo.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti recently allied with Aquino, who are rivals of Mazzaferro.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo un collaboratore di giustizia negli ultimi anni si è avvicinata ed alleata agli Aquino , rivali dei Mazzaferro .

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Macrì.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Lo Presti formed alliances with the Commisso 'ndrina in Canada.

descriptions: The Mazzaferro are closely linked by family ties to the Lo Presti.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono strettamente legati da vincoli di parentela ai Lo Presti di Bardonecchia.

descriptions: Midden Noord is a rival of AFCA.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Midden Noord

descriptions: F-side lists Midden Noord as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Midden Noord

Almighty Insane Popes Nation.description: The Almighty Insane Popes Nation is a European American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, formed in the late 1950s. Originally a Greek greaser gang, it adopted the name Popes, standing for 'Protecting Our People Eliminating Spics' (later changed to 'scum'). The gang expanded to include Polish, German, Irish, and Scandinavian members, and later invited Latino and black members. It split into North Side and South Side factions with different alliances.

descriptions: Insane North Side Popes are listed as rivals of the Gaylords in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Folk Nation , Insane Deuces, Simon City Royals , Insane Spanish Cobras , Maniac Latin Disciples , C-Notes, Insane North Side Popes

descriptions: North Side Popes rival Black P. Stones.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black P. Stones

descriptions: The Gaylords killed a Popes leader, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1975, Larkin was killed by members of the Almighty Gaylords

descriptions: North Side Popes rival Almighty Gaylords.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Almighty Gaylords

descriptions: North Side Popes rival Assyrian Kings.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Assyrian Kings

descriptions: North Side Popes rival Familia Stones.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Familia Stones

descriptions: South Side Popes hated the Two-Six gangs, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the south side branch, who hated the Satan Disciples and Two-Six gangs

descriptions: North Side Popes are allied with Imperial Gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Imperial Gangsters

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Insane Deuces

edges.evidence_quote: This small group had problems with the much larger Latin Kings gang on the north side

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation

edges.evidence_quote: the south side branch, who hated the Satan Disciples and Two-Six gangs

descriptions: The Popes associated with the Royals for protection, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: they began to associate with the Almighty Simon City Royals in an attempt to protect themselves

descriptions: South Side Popes rival the Spanish Cobras.
edges.evidence_quote: the South Side Popes' main rivals are the Spanish Cobras

descriptions: North Side Popes are allied with TAP Boyz.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: TAP Boyz

descriptions: Popes allied with Simon City Royals in 1975 after Gaylords killed Popes' leader.
edges.evidence_quote: the Popes decided to form an alliance with the Royals

descriptions: Sons of Silence had a turf war with Diablos Lobos in 1983.
edges.evidence_quote: A Sons of Silence member was charged with the murder of a member of the Diablos Lobos gang who was killed in an Aurora bar amidst a turf war involving the groups in 1983.

Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula.description: Sunni Islamist militant organization seeking to overthrow the Yemeni government and establish an Islamic emirate. Formed in 2009 from the merger of al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia. Based in Yemen, it is the most active al-Qaeda affiliate, conducting attacks in Yemen, Saudi Arabia, and the West. Its strength has waned due to internal struggles, civil war, and US drone strikes.

descriptions: AQAP is a direct affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda has the following direct affiliates: - Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula (AQAP)

descriptions: AQAP is a franchise of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Humayqani exploited his role in Alkarama to fundraise on behalf of al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula (AQAP).

descriptions: AQAP and al-Nusra Front were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies al-Nusra Front (2012–2017)

descriptions: AQAP is an affiliate of the central al-Qaeda network.
edges.evidence_quote: Part of the al-Qaeda network

descriptions: AQAP is an affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Part of the al-Qaeda network

descriptions: AQAP was formed by merging al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia.
edges.evidence_quote: Established in 2009 as a merger between al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia

descriptions: AQAP was formed by the merger of al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia.
edges.evidence_quote: Established in 2009 as a merger between al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia

descriptions: AQAP and AQIS are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies al-Qaeda in the Indian Subcontinent (2014–present)

descriptions: AQAP's newspaper featured content from other al-Qaeda affiliates, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: interviews and messages from leaders in Al-Qaeda core, Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, Al-Qaeda in the Indian Subcontinent and Al-Shabaab being featured

descriptions: AQAP and AQIM are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2009–present)

descriptions: AQAP's newspaper featured content from other al-Qaeda affiliates, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: interviews and messages from leaders in Al-Qaeda core, Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, Al-Qaeda in the Indian Subcontinent and Al-Shabaab being featured

descriptions: AQAP was formed by merging al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia.
edges.evidence_quote: Established in 2009 as a merger between al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia

descriptions: AQAP was formed by the merger of al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia.
edges.evidence_quote: Established in 2009 as a merger between al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia

descriptions: AQAP and al-Shabaab are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies al-Shabaab (2009–present)

descriptions: AQAP's newspaper featured content from other al-Qaeda affiliates, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: interviews and messages from leaders in Al-Qaeda core, Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, Al-Qaeda in the Indian Subcontinent and Al-Shabaab being featured

descriptions: AQAP and Ansar Al-Sharia cooperated to reestablish emirates in Yemen.
edges.evidence_quote: AQAP and Ansar Al-Sharia would reestablish their emirates across the country and significantly expand their power.

descriptions: Ansar Al-Sharia is allied with AQAP, as Tariq Al-Fadhli mediated between them.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2011, Ansar Al-Sharia gained the allyship of Tariq Al-Fadhli

descriptions: Ansar Al-Sharia is a subsidiary of AQAP.
edges.evidence_quote: AQAP created Ansar Al-Sharia as a subsidiary organization

descriptions: AQAP fought against the Houthis during the Yemeni civil war.
edges.evidence_quote: establishing a presence across multiple fronts in the civil war against the Houthis

descriptions: AQAP fought against the Houthis, allying with local tribes.
edges.evidence_quote: AQAP allied with the local, predominantly Sunni tribes to mount a fierce resistance against them

descriptions: AQAP and Houthis are alleged allies since 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Houthis (alleged, since 2022)

descriptions: AQAP and Islamic State-Yemen engaged in armed conflict over territory and interests.
edges.evidence_quote: a 'disagreement on properties, interests and positions' in July 2018 lead to a conflict between the two groups

descriptions: AQAP and ISIS-Y were in conflict but it ended by 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: AQAP's conflict with ISIS-Y had also dissipated in 2020, with no attacks being reported against either groups since February 2020.

descriptions: AQAP's media became more militaristic in response to the rise of the Islamic State, implying rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The rise of the Islamic State as well as the Houthis in 2014 led to an increase in militarism and sectarianism in AQAP's media.

descriptions: AQAP and Islamic State Yemen Province are opponents.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Non-state opponents Islamic State Yemen Province

descriptions: AQAP and the Southern Movement are opponents.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Non-state opponents Southern Movement Southern Transitional Council

descriptions: AQAP attacked STC forces and announced operations against them.
edges.evidence_quote: In response, AQAP launched one of their deadliest attacks in years against Security Belt Forces in September 2022 before announcing their own operation against the STC.

descriptions: AQIM listed al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula (2009–2017)

descriptions: Al-Shabaab has forged ties with AQAP.
edges.evidence_quote: has forged ties with other insurgent groups like AQIM and AQAP

descriptions: Houthis and AQAP had a ceasefire since 2022 and cooperated in attacks and intelligence.
edges.evidence_quote: Al Qaeda Arabia Province (AQAP) and Houthis had previously fought, but since 2022 they have had a ceasefire. As part of this ceasefire they have cooperated in attacks against Yemeni government, provided safe havens for each other in their territories, and cooperating in security and intelligence.

descriptions: IS lists AQAP as a non-state opponent, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Other non-state opponents: al-Qaeda, al-Nusra Front, Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula, Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, Al-Shabaab, Taliban, Hamas, Hezbollah, Houthis, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Syrian Democratic Forces, ...

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula (AGH) is listed as a non-state ally of Lashkar-e-Taiba.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Jaish-e-Mohammed, Pakistani Taliban, Hizbul Mujahideen, Harkat-ul-Jihad al-Islami, Harkat-ul-Mujahideen, Al-Badr, Al Rashid Trust, Indian Mujahideen, People’s Anti-Fascist Front, Muslim United Liberation Tigers of Assam, Al-Qaeda AQIS AGH

descriptions: The Barreca and Malacrinò split after internal disputes.
edges.evidence_quote: i rapporti tra Barreca e il cognato Malacrinò si incrinarono al punto da definire una vera scissione tra le due famiglie

descriptions: The Malacrinò are allied 'ndrine of the Iamonte.
edges.evidence_quote: I Moscato e i Malacrinò sono 'ndrine loro alleate.

Africa Corps.description: Russian paramilitary expeditionary force established by the Ministry of Defence in 2023, taking over Wagner Group operations in Africa. Provides military support, training, and counterterrorism cooperation in Sahel states, Libya, and Central African Republic. Sanctioned by the UK in 2024.

descriptions: Azawad Liberation Front shot down an Africa Corps helicopter, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: a helicopter belonging to the corps was shot down by the Azawad Liberation Front with both the crew and mobile fire group being transported by it killed

descriptions: Africa Corps cooperates with the Bears Brigade.
edges.evidence_quote: The formation cooperates with other government-controlled structures, like the " Bears Brigade " in Africa.

descriptions: Africa Corps rescued hostages from Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the corps rescued two employees of a Russian geological exploration company who were abducted by Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin in Niger two years earlier.

descriptions: Redut's leader is involved in the creation of Africa Corps, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Konstantin Mirzayants, a purported leader of Redut, is reportedly closely involved in the MoD's operations in Africa and the creation of the Africa Corps.

descriptions: Africa Corps subsumed and rebranded Wagner Group's structures in Africa.
edges.evidence_quote: The corps largely took over the operations of the Wagner Group in Africa, by subsuming and rebranding its structures.

descriptions: Wagner Group was merged into the Africa Corps.
edges.evidence_quote: By the following year, the Wagner Group in Africa was merged into the 'Africa Corps'.

On Leong Chinese Merchants Association.description: A tong society based in Chinatown, Manhattan, established in November 1893. It fought a violent war with the Hip Sing Tong for control of Chinatown's rackets and businesses. In recent years, it has been linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang. It has over 30,000 registered members, mostly with commercial or industrial backgrounds.

descriptions: Ghost Shadows was affiliated with On Leong Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang was affiliated with the On Leong Tong

descriptions: Hip Sing Tong and On Leong Tong were rivals engaged in violent Tong wars.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hip Sing Tong, along with their rivals the Four Brothers and the On Leong Tong , would be involved in violent Tong wars for control of Chinatown during the early 1900s.

descriptions: Hip Sing Tong and On Leong Tong fought violent attacks against each other during the Tong Wars.
edges.evidence_quote: This became known as the "Tong Wars", which were a series of violent attacks between two branches of the Tong Gang, the Hip Sing Tong and On Leong Tong.

descriptions: On Leong has been linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tong has been linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang led by Wing Yeung Chan

descriptions: On Leong and Hip Sing Tong fought a violent war for control of Chinatown's rackets and businesses.
edges.evidence_quote: the tong fought a violent war for control of Chinatown's rackets and businesses with the Hip Sing Tong

descriptions: Tse was briefly a member of On Leong before being expelled, indicating a conflict or falling out.
edges.evidence_quote: Tse joined the On Leong, but they expelled him shortly afterward and he joined the Hung Mun

descriptions: The Shimizu family sold its emblem to the head of Goryo-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Tanabe sold the Shimizu emblem for circa tens of millions of yen to Yasuo Takagi (head of the Goryo-kai and thereafter the Mio-gumi gang).

descriptions: The Shimizu family sold its emblem to the head of Mio-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Tanabe sold the Shimizu emblem for circa tens of millions of yen to Yasuo Takagi (head of the Goryo-kai and thereafter the Mio-gumi gang).

Aggi Crew.description: The Aggi Crew were a criminal drug gang based in the St Paul's district of Bristol, England. The name is an abbreviation of 'aggravated burglary'. They dominated Bristol's drug market in the 1990s until key members were jailed in 1998. After release, they attempted to reclaim territory from Yardie gangs, sparking a turf war that ended with their re-arrest.

descriptions: Aggi Crew demanded tax from Yardie gangs, leading to a turf war with shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: The yardies did not agree with this however and stormed a cafe run by the Aggis, which doubled as a front for an illegal gambling operation. Thus was started a small-scale turf war which included a number of shootings

descriptions: Aggi Crew demanded tax from Yardie gangs, leading to a turf war with shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: The yardies did not agree with this however and stormed a cafe run by the Aggis, which doubled as a front for an illegal gambling operation. Thus was started a small-scale turf war which included a number of shootings

descriptions: De Bernardo group is a satellite group operating under Mazzarella clan's command.
edges.evidence_quote: The De Bernardo group (led by Rosario De Bernardo, brother of Vincenzo 'o pisello', murdered in 2015) handled large-scale distribution of cocaine, crack, marijuana, and hashish, primarily in Somma Vesuviana.
